item.title: Mira Murati steps back into the spotlight, carefully
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: In the current environment, remaining heads down has diminishing returns; at some point, you have to make some noise just to remind the market you exist.

item.title: Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI’s returns
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Anthropic has been growing at a breakneck pace. The company announced that annualized revenue crossed $47 billion in May, up dramatically from roughly $9 billion at the end of 2025. That trajectory faces a real test, though.

item.title: Airbnb’s Brian Chesky plans to launch a new AI lab
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The Airbnb CEO said last year it hasn't struck an LLM partnership because existing products weren't quite ready.

item.title: Defense tech, AI, and fundraising take center stage at StrictlyVC Los Angeles on June 18
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: On Thursday, June 18, at The Aerospace Corporation Campus, investors, founders, and tech leaders will gather for an evening of conversation exploring some of the most consequential shifts taking place across venture capital, defense technology, artificial intelligence, and advanced industry. Secure your spot today.

item.title: Meta steals a tactic from Tesla and builds data centers in tents
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Meta may have found one way to slash its massive data center bill: tents.

item.title: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Poke, the startup that lets people use AI agents through simple text messages, has become the first AI agent approved for Apple’s Messages for Business platform.

item.title: Meta rolls out a new AI creator assistant on Facebook
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Creators often have to parse through charts and dashboards to understand their performance, but with the new AI assistant, they can get quick answers to questions like "When should I post?" and "What are people saying in my comments?"

item.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

item.title: Is Silicon Valley ready to put robots in people’s homes? Hello Robot is.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The California startup released the fourth-generation of its home assistance robot, Stretch.

item.title: Apple touts $1.4 trillion in App Store billings and sales, 90% without a commission
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple's App Store generated $1.4 trillion in sales, up from $1.3 trillion last year, with $149 billion in sales for digital goods.

item.title: Lovable signs multiyear deal with Google Cloud to up usage 5x, source says
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Lovable and Google signed an expanded multiyear deal that involves a 5x expansion of Lovable's footprint on Google Cloud, and expanded access to Anthropic Claude.

item.title: Alphabet’s record-breaking $85B raise for Google’s AI business is a helluva good signal
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: If Alphabet's record-breaking $85 billion stock sale signals investor appetite for AI-related offerings, we can see that investors are ready to chow.

item.title: Google’s Dreambeans, its weirdest-named AI tool to date, will turn your life into a cartoon
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Dreambeans is a curated list of AI-illustrated "stories" culled from the personal data in your Google account.

item.title: Amazon will show AI product images when you search for some reason
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Amazon will use visual search and AI to show AI-generated product images that match your search queries. The retailer says it will help guide users to products.

item.title: These two founders left Goldman and Meta to build voice AI for markets everyone else overlooked
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The startup's own stack for Africa and Middle East is now handling more than 17,000 calls per day.

item.title: Publishers will be able to opt out of AI Search, thanks to new regulation
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: U.K. regulators are requiring Google offer a tool allowing website publishers to opt-out of generative AI search features. The option will be tested in the U.K. then rolled out globally.

item.title: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: WhatsApp will charge businesses for using its AI agent based on token usage.

item.title: Coralogix raises $200M on bet that someone needs to watch the AI agents
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Coralogix is among a growing number of infrastructure firms betting that as AI systems move into production, demand will rise for tools that can monitor their behavior, troubleshoot failures, and provide the operational data needed to keep them running reliably.

item.title: Cyera eyes $12B valuation at 80x ARR multiple despite operating losses
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The cybersecurity company is nearing a $300 million round led by Evolution Equity Partners.

item.title: Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Uber's cutback has occurred after the company had reportedly encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible.
